info_gaps: no description of focal neuro deficit vs global drowsiness
any chest pressure, dyspnea, or back pain on direct questioning

resource.note: last monitored bed available — claim now

warnings: Vitals are reassuring but clinical gestalt is alarming — diaphoresis + ashen + drowsy + 'not himself' is a classic anginal-equivalent / occult shock presentation in a 64yo HTN male.
Do not be falsely reassured by HR 76 / BP 122 — patient is on amlodipine and HTN baseline may be much higher; this could be a relative hypotension.

hypotheses.name: Acute coronary syndrome (anginal-equivalent, no chest pain)
supporting_signals: weakness as anginal equivalent in older adults

hypotheses.name: Occult shock / sepsis (early, compensated)
supporting_signals: altered mental status (drowsy, slow to answer)

hypotheses.name: Acute stroke / posterior circulation event
rules_against: no facial droop / arm drift / aphasia documented yet

hypotheses.name: Aortic dissection / AAA (per similar case PC-013)
rules_against: no pulse or BP differential noted

immediate.action: 12-lead ECG within 10 min of arrival (deadline 10:45) + place on cardiac monitor
immediate.reason: 64yo HTN male with diaphoresis, ashen appearance, and altered mentation is an anginal-equivalent until proven otherwise.

immediate.action: Move to monitored bed now, IV access x2, draw troponin/CBC/BMP/lactate/coags/glucose, finger-stick glucose at bedside
immediate.reason: Undifferentiated sick-looking patient needs simultaneous ACS, stroke, sepsis, and metabolic workup; only 1 monitored bed left.

immediate.action: Physician bedside eval STAT for full neuro exam (NIHSS) and ASA 324mg chewable if ECG/exam supports ACS and no contraindication
immediate.reason: Altered mentation + sick gestalt requires MD eyes within minutes to disposition stroke vs ACS vs sepsis pathway.

monitor.action: Repeat full vitals q5min including orthostatics and bilateral arm BPs; recheck mental status
monitor.reason: Current vitals are reassuring but gestalt is not — trend will reveal compensated shock or evolving deficit.

monitor.action: Pending workup: troponin (repeat at 3h), lactate, ECG, CT head non-contrast if any focal deficit on exam
monitor.reason: Parallel workup needed because hypothesis space is wide (ACS / stroke / sepsis / dissection).

escalate_or_redirect.action: Notify on-call cardiology (ETA 15 min) for early review pending ECG/troponin
escalate_or_redirect.reason: Conservative bias: high-suspicion anginal-equivalent in older HTN male warrants early specialty awareness even before troponin returns.

explanation.summary: 64yo HTN male with vague weakness but striking clinical gestalt — ashen, diaphoretic, drowsy, wife says 'not him.' Vitals are deceptively normal, but this presentation in this demographic is high-risk for ACS anginal-equivalent, occult stroke, or early shock. ESI 2 (now) on R-ESI-T2 high-risk + altered mental status criteria. Door-to-ECG clock is running; claim the last monitored bed and run parallel ACS/stroke/sepsis workup.

citations.signal: high-risk situation + drowsy/lethargic patient meets emergent tier
age ≥40, HTN, diaphoresis as anginal-equivalent — triggers 12-lead ECG ≤10 min and ASA pathway
altered mentation with acute change per family — needs NIHSS and CT if focal deficit found
altered mental status is one sepsis screen criterion; need lactate to complete screen

checklist.task: 12-lead ECG and place on cardiac monitor
Move to monitored bed, IV x2, STAT labs (trop, lactate, BMP, CBC, coags, gluc)
Physician bedside eval with NIHSS and ACS assessment
Repeat vitals q5min including bilateral arm BPs
Direct-question patient/wife re: chest pressure, dyspnea, back pain, last-known-well time

why_this_tier: Clinical gestalt (ashen, diaphoretic, drowsy, family alarm) outweighs reassuring vitals in a 64yo HTN male.
Altered mental status alone qualifies for ESI 2 under R-ESI-T2.
ACS anginal-equivalent (R-ACS-01) frequently presents without chest pain in older adults — diaphoresis + weakness is enough to start the clock.
Conservative bias applied: when gestalt and vitals disagree, escalate.
would_downgrade_if: ECG normal AND first troponin negative AND glucose normal AND mental status returns to baseline AND bedside exam fully reassuring
Wife/patient identify a benign explanation (e.g., poor sleep, recent viral illness) and gestalt improves with observation
would_upgrade_if: BP drops, HR rises, or lactate elevated → shock pathway
Focal neuro deficit emerges on exam → stroke activation
